configurations.name: with_skill
configurations.description: Using industrial-deep-diagnostic v4.0 scripts (inspect.mjs, stats.mjs, template_visualize.py)

per_eval.notes: Perfect score — column type inference and time column detection work flawlessly on clean data
Perfect score — Pearson correlations match ground truth within 0.015 tolerance

1 false positive: row 9 flagged as anomaly (thickness=10.04 vs baseline mean=10.00). Cause: baseline variance is very low (std=0.02 in normal region), making z=2.0 already trigger at tiny deviations. The z-score method is sensitive when baseline is near-perfect. Recommendation: add a minimum deviation threshold (e.g., 5% of baseline mean) as a secondary filter.

analyst_observations: inspect.mjs: Production-ready for clean CSV data. Handles datetime detection and type inference correctly. Edge case: non-standard time column names would need keyword tuning.

stats.mjs: Correlation computation is accurate. Lag correlation works correctly. The z-score anomaly detection is too sensitive for processes with very low normal variation — a minimum effect size filter would reduce false positives.
Overall: Deterministic components pass 94.4% of assertions. The 1 failure is a tunable threshold issue, not a logic bug.
